name: Devil's Food Cake - Country Living Holidays
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour three 9-inch round cake pans.
2. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat butter, 3/4 cup sugar, salt, and vanilla until fluffy.
3. Sift cocoa powder, all-purpose flour, and baking soda into the butter mixture. Add water and beat at low speed until smooth.
4. In a medium bowl, beat egg whites until fluffy. Gradually add remaining 3/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form. Fold one-third at a time into the chocolate batter.
5. Divide batter evenly among prepared pans. Bake until centers spring back when touched (20-25 minutes).
6. Cool cakes in pans for 5 minutes. Transfer to a cooling rack and cool completely.
7. Prepare cocoa frosting as directed. Fill and assemble cake, spreading a thin, smooth layer over the top. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
8. Prepare chocolate glaze by placing the chilled cake on a cooling rack with a tray underneath. Pour glaze over the top of the cake, allowing it to flow down the sides. Smooth the sides.
9. Place the cake back in the refrigerator until the glaze is firm.
10. Decorate the top of the cake with cocoa powder and confectioners' sugar checkerboard just before serving (optional). Place 1-inch strips of thin cardboard gently onto the frosting, sift unsweetened cocoa over the top to fill spaces, then remove the strips. Sprinkle confectioners' sugar over the cake.
Cocoa Frosting: Beat together softened butter or margarine, confectioners' sugar, cocoa powder, cinnamon, vanilla, and milk in a medium bowl. Use to frost the cake.
Chocolate Glaze: Melt chocolate and butter in a double boiler until smooth. Stir in cold water and additional confectioners' sugar. Use to glaze the cake.
